zh.Kinderton Cum Hulme, Northwich, Cheshire: 英國英格蘭爾金德頓 姆 赫爾姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret de Dutton於1235年出生在英國爾金德頓時，她的父親Lord Thomas de Dutton21歲，母親Philippa Standon15歲。她於1253年與William Baron of Kinderton De Venables,在英國爾金德頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1293年在她的家鄉享年58歲。

zh-hans.Kinderton Cum Hulme, Northwich, Cheshire: 英国英格兰爾金德頓 姆 赫爾姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret de Dutton于1235年出生在英国爾金德頓时，她的父亲Lord Thomas de Dutton21岁，母亲Philippa Standon15岁。她于1253年与William Baron of Kinderton De Venables,在英国爾金德頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1293年在她的家乡享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cheshire, Lancashire, and Staffordshire): habitational name from either Dutton in Cheshire or Dutton in Lancashire. The first is named from Old English dūn ‘hill’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’; the second from the Old English personal name Dudd(a) (see Dodd 1) + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Dodd
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Dodd(e), Dudd(e), Old English Dodd(a), Dudd(a), a name of uncertain origin which remained in fairly widespread and frequent use from Lincolnshire to Devon and from Essex to Lancashire in England until the 14th century.

English: nickname from Middle English dod, a word of uncertain meaning, possibly a ‘lumpish, thickset person’ (compare modern English dialect dod ‘bunch or heap’), or by extension a ‘foolish person’ (compare Middle English dode-mused ‘stupid’), or perhaps a derivative of dodden ‘to shave (the head), to trim (hair)’, hence ‘the hairless or close-cropped one’.

English: possibly a modern variant of Daud or Dowd, the former arising from the Middle English personal name Daud(e), an extended form of Daw , and the latter from the Middle English personal name Doude, perhaps a side-form of either Dodde or of Daude, a pet form of Ralph .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret de Dutton was born in 1235, in Kinderton, Cheshire, England, her father, Lord Thomas de Dutton, was 21 and her mother, Philippa Standon, was 15. She married William Baron of Kinderton De Venables, in 1253, in Kinderton, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1293, in her hometown, at the age of 58.

fr.Kinderton Cum Hulme, Northwich, Cheshire: Kinderton cum Hulme, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret de Dutton est née en 1235, à Kinderton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord Thomas de Dutton, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Philippa Standon, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé William Baron of Kinderton De Venables, en 1253, à Kinderton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1293, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.Kinderton Cum Hulme, Northwich, Cheshire: Kinderton cum Hulme, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret de Dutton 1235 in Kinderton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord Thomas de Dutton 21 und ihre Mutter Philippa Standon 15. Sie hat 1253 in Kinderton, Cheshire, England William Baron of Kinderton De Venables, geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1293 im Alter von 58 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

ko.Kinderton Cum Hulme, Northwich, Cheshire: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 킨더턴 c 훌
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Dutton(이)가 1235년 영국 체실르 킨더턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Thomas de Dutton(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Philippa Standon(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1253년 영국 체실르 킨더턴에서 William Baron of Kinderton De Venables,(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1293년 그녀의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret de Dutton родилась в 1235 г. в местности киндиртон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Lord Thomas de Dutton было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Philippa Standon, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Baron of Kinderton De Venables, в 1253 г. в местности киндиртон, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1293 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 58 лет.
